channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Tackling Messi ‘huge challenge’ for Spain in World Cup final: Merino
item.description: Spanish midfielder says he has 'huge confidence' in his abilities after his impressive performances as a substitute.

item.title: Herbert leads British Open golf, equals record lowest score at a Major
item.description: Australian golfer Herbert ended the second round of the British Open with a two-shot lead on eight-under.

item.title: Star speedcubers square off at European championship
item.description: More than 1,200 speedcubers from 53 countries are descending are attending the European Speedcubing Championship.

item.title: ICE agent behind fatal shooting in Maine reportedly had troubled history
item.description: Relatives of David Brouillette, the agent who shot and killed a man in Maine, said he had record of violent behaviour.

item.title: Food emergency deepens across Uganda’s Karamoja
item.description: Food emergency deepens across Uganda’s Karamoja

item.title: World Cup winners to receive US-style championship rings, FIFA says
item.description: Players' rings will be limited edition of 2,026 individually numbered pieces, with the rest available for public sale.

item.title: Houthi supporters rally in Sanaa against ‘Saudi-led blockade’
item.description: Thousands of Houthi supporters rallied in Sanaa against what they described as the Saudi-led blockade against Yemen.

item.title: Suspected Somali pirates seize tanker near Yemen amid wave of hijackings
item.description: The seizure of the Astana is the latest in a rapidly growing wave of piracy sweeping the Gulf of Aden.

item.title: Top IRS lawyer to resign post amid disagreements with Trump administration
item.description: Reports indicate a controversial settlement deal and disputes over tax audits may have prompted Ken Kies to step down.

item.title: Israel, Gaza and the future of US power
item.description: Congressman Ro Khanna joins Sreenivasan Jain.

item.title: How an Italian sculptor created the iconic World Cup trophy
item.description: In 1970, an Italian sculptor designed the iconic World Cup trophy that’s become familiar to generations of fans.

item.title: At least two dead as heavy rains, floods strike central Texas
item.description: Officials in the southern state have seen new warning systems tested by the floods, after last year's deadly rainfall.

item.title: Will the Canadian wildfire smoke affect Spain vs Argentina World Cup final?
item.description: World Cup organisers are monitoring the situation 'closely', with US and FIFA presidents meeting on Friday.

item.title: At least three killed and hundreds displaced by heavy rain in Chile
item.description: Chilean President Jose Antonio Kast says that he is travelling to the Biobio region to survey flood damage.

item.title: All-time West Indies cricket great Garfield Sobers dies aged 89
item.description: Arguably the greatest all-rounder of the game, Sobers led the West Indies at his peak, breaking Test cricket records.

item.title: Germany’s Merz hails nuclear deterrence cooperation with France
item.description: The Franco-German cooperation is not a move to replace NATO’s nuclear umbrella, stresses German Chancellor.

item.title: Beyond LNG: Berlin’s red-carpet diplomacy signals a bigger bet on Algeria
item.description: Germany, stripped of Russian pipeline gas, received Algeria's president with military honours and talked about LNG.

item.title: Can the US and Iran reach a lasting deal to end the conflict?
item.description: Air strikes intensify raising fear of an all-out war.

item.title: Life after amputation: Gaza women find recovery through football
item.description: Palestinian women in Gaza who have lost limbs during Israel's genocidal war are using football as a way of recovering.

item.title: World Cup 2026: What did we learn?
item.description: World Cup 2026: What did we learn?

item.title: Burnham confirmed as Labour leader, vows new direction for UK
item.description: Britain's governing Labour Party confirmed as Andy Burnham as its new leader, clearing the way for him to become PM

item.title: Did the US just start a trade war with Brazil?
item.description: Did the US just start a trade war with Brazil?

item.title: Iranian forces: missiles and drones fired at US targets in Gulf States
item.description: Iranian forces: missiles and drones fired at US targets in Gulf States.

item.title: Apple regains top spot as world’s most valuable company
item.description: Apple regained world’s top spot with $4.88 trillion valuation, overtaking Nvidia which saw a 3.5% market value drop.

item.title: Amazon’s Zoox recalls self-driving vehicles amid emergency response issues
item.description: The recall is the latest development in a series of reports of autonomous vehicles impeding emergency response efforts.
